Apollo 11: Buzz Aldrin on the Moon. ### At the museum and online. # Alan Shepard: First American in Space. He became the first American and the second man in space on May 5, 1961, when he piloted the Mercury spacecraft *Freedom* 7 on a 490-kilometer (300-mile), 15-minute suborbital flight. A detail of Alan Shepard's spacesuit featuring the \"meatball\" patch, a blue circle with the text \"NASA\" on it and a red swoop. Shepard named the Mercury capsule he flew in \"Freedom 7,\" the number signifying the seven Mercury astronauts; NASA called the mission Mercury-Redstone 3 (MR-3).
